details.body: finding_summary: `record_task_run_start` still allows a failed historical project to reopen the same bead while another project already has that bead active. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:5168-5218`, the finalized-failed path calls `reopen_failed_exact_attempt(...)` and returns before the new cross-project duplicate check at `src/adapters/fs.rs:5277-5283` runs. A concrete case is: project A fails on bead X, project B is now running bead X, then `run resume` on project A succeeds and creates a second active lineage for bead X. Because milestone reconciliation collapses to the latest run per bead (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:967-999`), that parallel-active state can be hidden from progress accounting instead of surfacing as corruption. affected_files: [`src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`]. remediation_hint: reject `reopen_failed_exact_attempt` whenever `running_attempts_for_bead` contains another project, or move the same-bead active-project guard ahead of all finalized-attempt reopen paths.

finding_summary: `task create` now treats legacy non-terminal lineage rows with `run_id: null` as active duplicates even though `run start` auto-repairs those rows to `Failed` before checking conflicts. The new `FsTaskRunLineageStore::active_task_runs_for_bead` helper (`src/adapters/fs.rs:5089-5108`) reads/collapses the file without applying the legacy repair that `record_task_run_start` performs first (`src/adapters/fs.rs:5144-5148`). `execute_create_from_bead` then rejects solely on that helper (`src/cli/project.rs:574-579`), so an upgraded workspace with an old runless row will get a false duplicate-active rejection and cannot recreate the bead even though start-time semantics would have allowed progress. affected_files: `src/adapters/fs.rs:5089-5108,5144-5148`, `src/cli/project.rs:574-579`. remediation_hint: make the read-only helper mirror start-time legacy handling, either by excluding non-terminal `run_id: null` rows from the returned active set or by running the same upgrade-repair logic before duplicate detection.

finding_summary: the new orphaned-lineage handling only special-cases a missing project record. If the project directory still exists but `projects/<id>/run.json` is missing or corrupt, `reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation` falls through to `FsRunSnapshotStore.read_run_snapshot` (`src/cli/project.rs:606-607`), which bubbles a raw `CorruptRecord` from `src/adapters/fs.rs:1983-2004` instead of the promised remediation hint about restoring the project or repairing `milestones/<id>/task-runs.ndjson`. That leaves a half-deleted active project in the same opaque failure mode the bead was supposed to make actionable. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:594-607`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:1983-2004`. remediation_hint: wrap missing/corrupt run-snapshot reads in the duplicate-bead check and rethrow them with the same operator guidance used for the missing-project case.

finding_summary: Create-time duplicate detection reads `task-runs.ndjson` even when the milestone snapshot still has `pending_lineage_reset`, so rematerialized plans can be blocked by stale same-bead lineage that `record_bead_start` would clear before any start.
affected_files: ["src/cli/project.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
file_refs: ["src/cli/project.rs:351", "src/cli/project.rs:440", "src/cli/project.rs:583-593", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:411-431", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2988-3000"]
remediation_hint: Before `reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation` trusts lineage, honor `pending_lineage_reset` the same way `record_bead_start` does, or skip the create-time scan until the reset has been cleared under the milestone write lock.

finding_summary: `project create-from-bead` still permits a parallel project when the only existing active lineage row is a legacy `run_id: null` attempt. The new read-only helper explicitly drops every non-terminal row without a run ID (`src/adapters/fs.rs:5089-5104`), `execute_create_from_bead` relies on that helper for its duplicate guard (`src/cli/project.rs:587-597`), and the integration test now locks that behavior in as success (`tests/cli.rs:13549-13594`). In an upgraded workspace with one of these legacy active rows, create-time validation will silently ignore the existing same-bead attempt, mint a second project, and leave the ambiguity to be "repaired" only later on a start-time write. That misses the bead's stated goal of surfacing or blocking inconsistent active lineage at create time instead of allowing another task to be created.
remediation_hint: Treat non-terminal runless rows as an inconsistent/orphaned active lineage that blocks creation with a repair hint, or run the same legacy-row repair before duplicate detection so create-time behavior matches start-time behavior.

## Finding Summary
`project create-from-bead` still has a TOCTOU race around the new duplicate-active-bead guard. `execute_create_from_bead` performs the duplicate scan before `claim_bead_in_br` (`src/cli/project.rs:444-455`, `src/cli/project.rs:592-597`), and the helper it calls is only an unlocked read of `task-runs.ndjson` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:5095-5108`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:4325-4359`). Another operator can start or create the same bead after this read and before the claim/project side effects land, so the branch can still mint a second project for an already-active bead.

## Affected Files
- `src/cli/project.rs`
- `src/adapters/fs.rs`

## Remediation Hint
Serialize create-time validation with the lineage mutation path, or write a provisional same-bead reservation under the task-run lock before releasing control back to the async claim/create flow, so the duplicate check and subsequent side effects are atomic.

## Finding Summary
The new `reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation` branch treats `RunStatus::NotStarted` as a normal `DuplicateActiveBead` conflict (`src/cli/project.rs:654-664`). That state is already inconsistent if `task-runs.ndjson` contains an active row, so it should be surfaced as orphaned/corrupt lineage with a repair hint instead. Right now the emitted message sends the operator to `ralph-burning run resume`, but `run resume` explicitly rejects `not_started` projects (`src/cli/run.rs:3820-3824`). In the exact repair case this bead is supposed to handle, the CLI points users at a command that cannot work.

## Affected Files
- `src/cli/project.rs`
- `src/cli/run.rs`

## Remediation Hint
Handle `RunStatus::NotStarted` like the other broken-lineage cases: return a repair-oriented error that names the stale lineage row and tells the operator to repair `milestones/<id>/task-runs.ndjson` or restore the project snapshot, instead of emitting `DuplicateActiveBead`.

finding_summary: The new orphaned/inconsistent same-bead lineage handling only runs during `project create-from-bead`; `run start` still short-circuits to `DuplicateActiveBead` as soon as it sees another project's non-terminal row, without checking whether that referenced project/run state is missing or corrupt. In `record_bead_start` the run-start path goes straight into `record_task_run_start` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3033-3041`), and `record_task_run_start` now returns `DuplicateActiveBead` immediately for a different project on the same bead (`src/adapters/fs.rs:5190-5195`). The new repair/remediation logic lives only in `reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation` (`src/cli/project.rs:589-709`). As a result, a stale lineage row pointing at a deleted or unreadable project still blocks `run start`/`run resume` with a misleading "resume that project" error instead of the required remediation hint, so create/start semantics are still not aligned.
remediation_hint: Move the project/run-snapshot validation behind active same-bead conflicts into a shared helper and use it from both create-time duplicate checks and the run-start lineage path before emitting `DuplicateActiveBead`.

[src/cli/project.rs:444-478,617-668] `execute_create_from_bead` only preflights active `task-runs.ndjson` entries. A bead-backed project that already exists but has never started has no lineage row, so a second `project create-from-bead --project-id ...` for the same bead slips past the new guard and falls into `claim_bead_in_br`. That path returns a generic br-claim failure and transitions the milestone controller to `needs_operator`, even though this is just the duplicate-not-started case the bead policy is supposed to reject cleanly. The command should detect an existing same-bead project before the br claim and return a targeted duplicate-bead error naming that project instead of taking the operator-recovery path.

[src/adapters/fs.rs:4651-4659,5147-5191] `record_task_run_start` still rewrites every non-terminal `run_id: None` lineage row to `failed` before it evaluates same-bead conflicts. In an upgraded workspace with a legacy active row, `run start` on a second project for the same bead will silently auto-fail the old active entry and proceed, instead of surfacing the repair guidance that `project create-from-bead` now emits for the same corrupted/orphaned state. That leaves create/start semantics misaligned and still lets corrupted same-bead lineage be bypassed rather than rejected.

`src/cli/project.rs:634-663` only special-cases `RunStatus::NotStarted` when an existing project already owns the same bead. If the prior bead-backed project is in `failed`, `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation()` returns `Ok(())`, so `execute_create_from_bead()` falls through to `claim_bead_in_br` instead of forcing the operator back to `ralph-burning project select <id>` + `ralph-burning run resume`. That violates the bead's retry policy for failed/stopped attempts and can still yield a parallel task or an unrelated BR-claim failure. Failed same-bead projects need the same targeted create-time rejection/resume guidance as the active-lineage helper.

`src/cli/project.rs:444-446` relies on the new task-runs lock to make duplicate-bead validation atomic, but `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:413-425` and `3140-3147` still clear `task-runs.ndjson` directly during pending-lineage-reset handling without taking that same lock. That path is reachable from `record_bead_start()` at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3172-3175`, so a concurrent lineage-resetting operation can rewrite the file while `create-from-bead` believes it has exclusive access. The new lock therefore does not actually close the TOCTOU window it was added for; lineage reset needs to go through the lineage store or acquire the task-runs lock before truncating the file.

src/cli/project.rs:650-666 allows `RunStatus::Running` to fall through in `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation`. If a bead-backed project is still running but its `milestones/<id>/task-runs.ndjson` row has been lost or cleared, `reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation` sees no active lineage and this code will mint a second project anyway. The bead prompt explicitly says orphaned/inconsistent state must fail closed with remediation guidance instead of creating a shadow project, so this path reintroduces duplicate active-bead creation under a plausible repair/corruption scenario. Treat a same-bead project in `running` state without matching active lineage as a repairable error and add a CLI test for a running `run.json` plus empty lineage.

src/cli/project.rs:629-665 relies on `find_existing_bead_project`, but that helper only returns one project and prefers the default project ID when several projects reference the same bead (src/cli/project.rs:702-723). After this branch's new 'allow create after terminal attempt' behavior, multiple same-bead projects are a normal state. If the original/default project is completed but a later retry project is `failed` or `not_started`, the create path inspects the completed one and incorrectly allows yet another project; if there is no default project, it aborts with a generic 'multiple projects already reference bead' error even when every prior attempt is terminal. `src/cli/milestone.rs:1146-1172` inherits the same helper, so milestone adoption can also select the wrong project. Replace the single-project lookup with a scan over all matching projects that prefers non-terminal states and only allows creation when every matching project is terminal, with coverage for 'completed default + failed retry' and 'multiple terminal retries'.

finding_summary: `active_same_bead_conflict_error` never verifies that the project named by the active lineage row actually belongs to the same milestone/bead. In [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2649), the code treats any readable `project.toml` as valid and then, at lines 2682-2717, turns the row into `DuplicateActiveBead` / `run resume` guidance based only on `run.json` status. If `task-runs.ndjson` is corrupt and points at another bead's project or a manual project with no matching `task_source`, `task create` and `run start` will block with the wrong remediation and can send the operator into an unrelated project instead of surfacing an orphaned-lineage repair error. affected_files: `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2649-2717`. remediation_hint: after `read_project_record`, validate `task_source.milestone_id` and `task_source.bead_id` with `milestone_bead_refs_match`; if they do not match, return the same repair-style `RunStartFailed` used for missing/corrupt/orphaned lineage.

finding_summary: the new same-bead project selection logic silently picks one project when multiple matching projects exist, instead of failing closed on ambiguous historical duplicates. In [src/cli/project.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:662), `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation` sorts all matching projects and uses only the first row (lines 673-704) to decide whether to block with `run start`/`run resume` guidance; in [src/cli/project.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:782), `find_existing_bead_project` does the same and returns the first sorted project at lines 793-796. That means a workspace containing two retryable/non-terminal same-bead projects will now direct `task create` or `milestone run` to an arbitrary project chosen by status/default-id/lexical order, rather than surfacing that the state is ambiguous and needs operator repair. This is especially risky because these duplicate states are exactly what the branch is hardening against, and older workspaces can already contain them. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:662-704`, `src/cli/project.rs:782-796`. remediation_hint: only auto-select when there is a single unambiguous candidate (or one candidate backed by the active lineage); otherwise return an explicit ambiguity/repair error listing the competing project ids.

Finding: `ensure_project_for_controller` still retries only on `AppError::DuplicateProject` (`src/cli/milestone.rs:1169-1195`), but `execute_create_from_bead` now rejects same-bead duplicates earlier via `DuplicateBeadProject` / `DuplicateActiveBead` before it ever reaches the generic `project_exists` branch (`src/cli/project.rs:444-457`, `src/cli/project.rs:664-680`). That regresses the existing duplicate-project race recovery: if another process creates or starts the bead-backed project after the initial `find_existing_bead_project()` scan but before `execute_create_from_bead()` runs, milestone execution now aborts instead of re-scanning and adopting the already-created project. A second `find_existing_bead_project()` call would recover that project (`src/cli/project.rs:837-950`), so the new error shapes need to be folded into the same recovery path. Affected files: `src/cli/milestone.rs`, `src/cli/project.rs`. Remediation hint: treat `DuplicateBeadProject` / `DuplicateActiveBead` (and any equivalent same-bead create-time duplicate outcome) like the old `DuplicateProject` race and re-run `find_existing_bead_project()` before failing the milestone command.

finding_summary: `find_existing_bead_project` now treats an all-terminal same-bead history as fatal ambiguity whenever more than one completed project matches (src/cli/project.rs:909-921), even though the create path explicitly allows that state (src/cli/project.rs:640-645). Because `ensure_project_for_controller` calls this helper before it can create or adopt anything (src/cli/milestone.rs:1146-1158), `milestone run` can no longer recover after a restart/controller desync when a bead has a completed default project plus a completed retry; the command fails instead of selecting a recoverable project or creating a fresh one. affected_files: src/cli/project.rs, src/cli/milestone.rs. remediation_hint: keep all-terminal same-bead histories recoverable here too by preferring a deterministic completed candidate (for example the default or newest completed project) or by falling through to fresh creation instead of raising ambiguity.

finding_summary: The new create-time duplicate guard reuses `matching_bead_project_ids`, whose legacy fallback treats any task-source-free project whose id equals the bead’s default generated id as a same-bead match (src/cli/project.rs:759-805). `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation` then blocks `task create` based on that inferred match (src/cli/project.rs:623-690), so an unrelated manual project named like the default bead id is now misclassified as an existing bead task and the operator is told to start/resume it instead of creating the requested project. affected_files: src/cli/project.rs. remediation_hint: restrict the default-id/no-task-source fallback to explicit recovery paths for legacy bead-backed projects, or require actual task-source/lineage evidence before the create-time blocker fires.

finding_summary: `find_existing_bead_project` treats any project whose id matches the default bead id and has `task_source: None` as a recoverable bead-backed project, so `milestone run` can adopt an unrelated manual project.
affected_files: src/cli/project.rs:766-817, src/cli/milestone.rs:1146-1164, src/cli/milestone.rs:1183-1196
remediation_hint: only recover a legacy no-`task_source` project when there is durable evidence that it really belongs to the bead (for example a matching lineage row or migrated linkage metadata); otherwise return `None` and let the controller create a fresh bead-backed project.

finding_summary: `active_same_bead_conflict_error` treats any linked project with `run.json` status `running` or `paused` as proof that the lineage row is still the live attempt, but it never verifies that the snapshot still points at the same `run_id` / attempt as `task-runs.ndjson`.
affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2617-2730
remediation_hint: compare the lineage row against `active_run` for running snapshots and `interrupted_run` for paused/failed snapshots; if the run identity or `started_at` disagrees, surface a repair-style inconsistency error instead of `DuplicateActiveBead`.

finding_summary: `project create-from-bead` still ignores lineage-backed legacy same-bead projects that lack `task_source`, so an older failed or paused retry can bypass the duplicate check and mint a parallel project. `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation` hard-codes `SameBeadProjectMatchMode::ExplicitTaskSourceOnly` at `src/cli/project.rs:623-636`, and `matching_bead_project_ids` immediately returns before consulting lineage evidence at `src/cli/project.rs:805-824`. That diverges from `find_existing_bead_project`, which does recover legacy default-named bead projects from `task-runs.ndjson` evidence at `src/cli/project.rs:927-934` and `src/cli/project.rs:3661-3684`. In practice, a legacy `task-ms-alpha-bead-2` project with `run.json` in `failed` state plus a matching historical lineage row will currently pass create-time validation and create a second project instead of steering the operator to resume the existing one.

finding_summary: Milestone-controller recovery still fails closed when the open same-bead lineage points at a failed project snapshot. `active_same_bead_conflict_error` intentionally treats `RunStatus::Failed` as a resumable retry case at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2834-2854`, but `find_existing_bead_project` converts that result into a hard `MilestoneOperationFailed` instead of returning the project id at `src/cli/project.rs:886-903`. That means `ensure_project_for_controller` cannot adopt the existing project at `src/cli/milestone.rs:1148-1159`, even though `load_run_action` would immediately map a failed snapshot to `Resume` at `src/cli/milestone.rs:1243-1252`. If controller state loses `active_task_id`, a normal failed-retry bead becomes unrecoverable until an operator intervenes.

finding_summary: Failed/paused same-bead projects are treated as resumable based only on `RunStatus`, so `project create-from-bead` and `milestone run` can point operators at a project that `run resume` cannot actually resume.
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:665-675
- src/cli/project.rs:954-967
- src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs:130-156
- src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:786-793
details: `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation` and `find_existing_bead_project` now block or adopt any same-bead project in `failed` / `paused` state without checking whether resumable metadata still exists. But `RunSnapshot::validate_semantics` explicitly allows failed/paused snapshots with `interrupted_run == None`, and the resume engine then fails when neither `interrupted_run` nor `active_run` is present. In that state this branch will reject a fresh task and tell the operator to `run resume`, or auto-adopt the broken project for `milestone run`, only for the next step to fail immediately.
remediation_hint: When choosing a failed/paused same-bead project, verify that resume metadata exists (or can be reconstructed from the journal) before steering the user to `run resume`; otherwise surface a repair-required error instead of treating that project as the canonical retry target.

finding_summary: `milestone run` still reuses stale same-bead lineage after a pending lineage reset, so controller execution can adopt work that `task create` would now correctly discard.
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:351-355
- src/cli/project.rs:859-920
- src/cli/milestone.rs:1148-1168
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2541-2551
details: `execute_create_from_bead` was updated to call `load_snapshot_clearing_pending_lineage_reset` before duplicate checks, but `ensure_project_for_controller` still calls `find_existing_bead_project` directly, and `find_existing_bead_project` reads `task-runs.ndjson` / same-bead project snapshots without first applying that reset. If a plan update has set `pending_lineage_reset` and the controller no longer has `active_task_id`, `milestone run` can adopt an old project or active lineage row that the create path would now clear, resuming stale work against an outdated plan.
remediation_hint: Clear `pending_lineage_reset` before any same-bead project scan/adoption path as well, not just in `execute_create_from_bead`.
